title: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Spanish Armada
rawPassageText:
READING PASSAGE 1
You should spend about 20 minutes on Questions 1-13, which are based on Reading Passage 1 below.

The Spanish Armada

A

In 1588, King Philip II of Spain sent a large fleet towards England. This fleet became known as the Spanish Armada. Philip's aim was not simply to fight a sea battle. He wanted the Armada to sail through the English Channel, join the army of the Duke of Parma in the Netherlands and help carry Spanish troops across the water to England. The plan was partly religious and partly political. Protestant England had supported Dutch rebels against Catholic Spain, and English privateers had also attacked Spanish ships carrying wealth from the Americas.

B

The Armada was impressive to look at. It contained about 130 ships and thousands of sailors, soldiers and guns. Many Spanish vessels were built to carry troops as well as weapons, so they were often high-sided and heavy. Spanish commanders hoped to keep the fleet together in a defensive crescent formation, with the weaker ships protected inside the curve. This formation made the Armada difficult to break apart, but it also made quick changes of direction harder. The English ships were generally smaller and more manoeuvrable, and their commanders preferred to fire from a distance rather than close in for boarding.

C

The first clashes in the Channel did not destroy the Armada. English ships followed and attacked, but they found it difficult to stop the Spanish fleet while it remained in formation. The turning point came near Calais, where the Armada anchored and waited for Parma's army. The English sent burning fireships towards the Spanish fleet at night. The fireships caused panic, forcing many Spanish ships to cut their anchors and scatter. When battle resumed near Gravelines, the English were able to attack a fleet that was no longer arranged as carefully as before.

D

Even after Gravelines, the Armada was not completely defeated in battle. Bad weather and shortage of supplies made it impossible for the Spanish to return through the Channel, so the fleet sailed north around Scotland and Ireland. This route was dangerous. Many damaged ships were driven onto rocks or beaches by storms, and sailors who reached land were often killed or captured. More Spanish ships were lost during this retreat than in the fighting with the English fleet itself.

E

The defeat of the Armada became a powerful story in England. It was later presented as proof that a small Protestant island had been saved from a mighty Catholic empire. Queen Elizabeth I's speeches and portraits helped turn the event into part of national memory. However, historians are careful not to describe the result as a simple miracle or as the immediate end of Spanish power. Spain remained a major naval and military force after 1588, and war between England and Spain continued for many years.

F

The Armada is still remembered because it combined planning, technology, leadership and chance. Spanish strategy depended on joining a fleet and an army at exactly the right moment, but communication across sea and land was slow. English tactics made good use of faster ships and long-range gunfire, but they did not alone decide the outcome. Weather, damaged anchors, poor coordination and the difficulty of supplying a huge fleet all played a part. The event shows how a famous victory can be shaped by practical problems as much as by courage.

Questions 1-7

Complete the notes below.

Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.

Write your answers in boxes 1-7 on your answer sheet.

The Spanish Armada

Philip II sent a large 1 .................... towards England in 1588. He wanted it to join Parma's 2 .................... in the Netherlands before carrying troops to England. The Armada sailed in a defensive 3 .................... formation, but this made it harder to change direction quickly. Near Calais, the English used burning 4 .................... to disturb the Spanish ships. After Gravelines, the Armada could not return through the 5 .................... . Many ships were then damaged or destroyed by 6 .................... during the retreat. Later, the Armada became part of England's national 7 .................... .

Questions 8-13

Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 1?

In boxes 8-13 on your answer sheet, write

YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer
NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this

8 Philip II intended the Armada to help transport soldiers to England.
9 The English ships were generally larger and less manoeuvrable than the Spanish ships.
10 Most Spanish ships were destroyed during the first clashes in the Channel.
11 The fireships near Calais forced some Spanish ships to abandon their anchors.
12 Queen Elizabeth I personally directed the English ships during the battle near Gravelines.
13 The writer suggests that weather was one of several factors that helped shape the Armada's defeat.
rawAnswerKey:
Question 1: Philip II sent a large .................... towards England in 1588.

Correct Answer: fleet

Accepted Answers: fleet

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Spanish Armada

Exact Portion: In 1588, King Philip II of Spain sent a large fleet towards England.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ fleet เพราะช่องว่างต้องเติมคำจากหลักฐานใน passage: “In 1588, King Philip II of Spain sent a large fleet towards England.” sent a large ____ towards England ตรงกับ sent a large fleet towards England คำนี้พอดีกับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคและเป็นคำเดียวตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY

Paraphrased Vocabulary: sent = sent; large = large; answer signal = fleet

Question 2: He wanted it to join Parma's .................... in the Netherlands before carrying troops to England.

Correct Answer: army

Accepted Answers: army

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Spanish Armada

Exact Portion: He wanted the Armada to sail through the English Channel, join the army of the Duke of Parma in the Netherlands and help carry Spanish troops across the water to England.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ army เพราะช่องว่างต้องเติมคำจากหลักฐานใน passage: “He wanted the Armada to sail through the English Channel, join the army of the Duke of Parma in the Netherlands and help carry Spanish troops across the water to England.” join Parma's ____ in the Netherlands ตรงกับ join the army of the Duke of Parma in the Netherlands คำนี้พอดีกับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคและเป็นคำเดียวตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY

Paraphrased Vocabulary: wanted it to = wanted the Armada to; carrying troops = carry Spanish troops; answer signal = army

Question 3: The Armada sailed in a defensive .................... formation, but this made it harder to change direction quickly.

Correct Answer: crescent

Accepted Answers: crescent

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Spanish Armada

Exact Portion: Spanish commanders hoped to keep the fleet together in a defensive crescent formation

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ crescent เพราะช่องว่างต้องเติมคำจากหลักฐานใน passage: “Spanish commanders hoped to keep the fleet together in a defensive crescent formation” defensive ____ formation ตรงกับ defensive crescent formation คำนี้พอดีกับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคและเป็นคำเดียวตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY

Paraphrased Vocabulary: sailed in = keep the fleet together in; formation = formation; answer signal = crescent

Question 4: Near Calais, the English used burning .................... to disturb the Spanish ships.

Correct Answer: fireships

Accepted Answers: fireships

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Spanish Armada

Exact Portion: The English sent burning fireships towards the Spanish fleet at night.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ fireships เพราะช่องว่างต้องเติมคำจากหลักฐานใน passage: “The English sent burning fireships towards the Spanish fleet at night.” used burning ____ ตรงกับ sent burning fireships คำนี้พอดีกับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคและเป็นคำเดียวตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY

Paraphrased Vocabulary: used = sent; Spanish ships = Spanish fleet; answer signal = fireships

Question 5: After Gravelines, the Armada could not return through the .................... .

Correct Answer: Channel

Accepted Answers: Channel

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Spanish Armada

Exact Portion: Bad weather and shortage of supplies made it impossible for the Spanish to return through the Channel

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ Channel เพราะช่องว่างต้องเติมคำจากหลักฐานใน passage: “Bad weather and shortage of supplies made it impossible for the Spanish to return through the Channel” could not return through the ____ ตรงกับ impossible to return through the Channel คำนี้พอดีกับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคและเป็นคำเดียวตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY

Paraphrased Vocabulary: could not = made it impossible; return = return; answer signal = Channel

Question 6: Many ships were then damaged or destroyed by .................... during the retreat.

Correct Answer: storms

Accepted Answers: storms

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Spanish Armada

Exact Portion: Many damaged ships were driven onto rocks or beaches by storms

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ storms เพราะช่องว่างต้องเติมคำจากหลักฐานใน passage: “Many damaged ships were driven onto rocks or beaches by storms” damaged or destroyed by ____ during retreat ตรงกับ driven onto rocks or beaches by storms คำนี้พอดีกับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคและเป็นคำเดียวตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY

Paraphrased Vocabulary: damaged or destroyed = driven onto rocks or beaches; during the retreat = during this retreat; answer signal = storms

Question 7: Later, the Armada became part of England's national .................... .

Correct Answer: memory

Accepted Answers: memory

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Spanish Armada

Exact Portion: Queen Elizabeth I's speeches and portraits helped turn the event into part of national memory.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ memory เพราะช่องว่างต้องเติมคำจากหลักฐานใน passage: “Queen Elizabeth I's speeches and portraits helped turn the event into part of national memory.” became part of England's national ____ ตรงกับ part of national memory คำนี้พอดีกับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคและเป็นคำเดียวตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY

Paraphrased Vocabulary: later = helped turn the event; England's = national in England context; answer signal = memory

Question 8: Philip II intended the Armada to help transport soldiers to England.

Correct Answer: YES

Accepted Answers: YES

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Spanish Armada

Exact Portion: He wanted the Armada to sail through the English Channel, join the army of the Duke of Parma in the Netherlands and help carry Spanish troops across the water to England.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ YES เพราะข้อความในโจทย์สอดคล้องกับหลักฐานใน passage: “He wanted the Armada to sail through the English Channel, join the army of the Duke of Parma in the Netherlands and help carry Spanish troops across the water to England.” help transport soldiers = help carry Spanish troops across the water to England ประเด็นสำคัญคือคำในโจทย์ถูก paraphrase เป็นคำใน passage แต่ความหมายยังเหมือนเดิม จึงเลือก YES

Paraphrased Vocabulary: intended = wanted; soldiers = troops; transport = carry; answer signal = YES

Question 9: The English ships were generally larger and less manoeuvrable than the Spanish ships.

Correct Answer: NO

Accepted Answers: NO

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Spanish Armada

Exact Portion: The English ships were generally smaller and more manoeuvrable

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ NO เพราะข้อความในโจทย์ขัดกับหลักฐานใน passage: “The English ships were generally smaller and more manoeuvrable” ข้อบอก English ships larger and less manoeuvrable แต่ passage บอก smaller and more manoeuvrable จุดตัดสินคือ passage ให้ข้อมูลตรงข้ามกับโจทย์ ไม่ใช่แค่ให้รายละเอียดเพิ่ม จึงต้องตอบ NO

Paraphrased Vocabulary: larger = contradicted by smaller; less manoeuvrable = contradicted by more manoeuvrable; answer signal = NO

Question 10: Most Spanish ships were destroyed during the first clashes in the Channel.

Correct Answer: NO

Accepted Answers: NO

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Spanish Armada

Exact Portion: The first clashes in the Channel did not destroy the Armada.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ NO เพราะข้อความในโจทย์ขัดกับหลักฐานใน passage: “The first clashes in the Channel did not destroy the Armada.” ข้อบอก most ships destroyed during first clashes แต่ passage บอก first clashes did not destroy the Armada จุดตัดสินคือ passage ให้ข้อมูลตรงข้ามกับโจทย์ ไม่ใช่แค่ให้รายละเอียดเพิ่ม จึงต้องตอบ NO

Paraphrased Vocabulary: first clashes = first clashes; destroyed = destroy; answer signal = NO

Question 11: The fireships near Calais forced some Spanish ships to abandon their anchors.

Correct Answer: YES

Accepted Answers: YES

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Spanish Armada

Exact Portion: The fireships caused panic, forcing many Spanish ships to cut their anchors and scatter.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ YES เพราะข้อความในโจทย์สอดคล้องกับหลักฐานใน passage: “The fireships caused panic, forcing many Spanish ships to cut their anchors and scatter.” forced some ships to abandon anchors = forcing many Spanish ships to cut their anchors and scatter ประเด็นสำคัญคือคำในโจทย์ถูก paraphrase เป็นคำใน passage แต่ความหมายยังเหมือนเดิม จึงเลือก YES

Paraphrased Vocabulary: some = many; abandon = cut; near Calais = came near Calais; answer signal = YES

Question 12: Queen Elizabeth I personally directed the English ships during the battle near Gravelines.

Correct Answer: NOT GIVEN

Accepted Answers: NOT GIVEN

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Spanish Armada

Exact Portion: Queen Elizabeth I's speeches and portraits helped turn the event into part of national memory.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ NOT GIVEN เพราะ passage มีข้อมูลใกล้เคียงว่า “Queen Elizabeth I's speeches and portraits helped turn the event into part of national memory.” แต่ไม่ได้ยืนยันรายละเอียดเฉพาะที่โจทย์ถาม passage กล่าวถึง speeches and portraits ของ Queen Elizabeth I แต่ไม่ได้บอกว่าเธอ personally directed ships at Gravelines หรือไม่ ดังนั้นไม่มีหลักฐานพอจะตอบ YES/NO หรือ TRUE/FALSE จึงเป็น NOT GIVEN

Paraphrased Vocabulary: personally directed ships = no information; battle near Gravelines = no information about Elizabeth directing it; answer signal = NOT GIVEN

Question 13: The writer suggests that weather was one of several factors that helped shape the Armada's defeat.

Correct Answer: YES

Accepted Answers: YES

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading Feb 2026 Passage 1 - The Spanish Armada

Exact Portion: Weather, damaged anchors, poor coordination and the difficulty of supplying a huge fleet all played a part.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ YES เพราะข้อความในโจทย์สอดคล้องกับหลักฐานใน passage: “Weather, damaged anchors, poor coordination and the difficulty of supplying a huge fleet all played a part.” weather was one of several factors ตรงกับ Weather, damaged anchors, poor coordination and supply difficulty all played a part ประเด็นสำคัญคือคำในโจทย์ถูก paraphrase เป็นคำใน passage แต่ความหมายยังเหมือนเดิม จึงเลือก YES

Paraphrased Vocabulary: one of several factors = all played a part; helped shape defeat = played a part; answer signal = YES
